title: The Router Network
concept: A router network evaluates each token and routes it to the most appropriate expert (e.g. routing math tokens to the math expert), saving compute.

exercises.prompt: How does a router network save computing power in an AI model?

options.text: By rewriting the user's prompt to be shorter.
By routing tokens only to the most appropriate experts.
By shutting down the model when it gets too hot.

exercises.explain: Just like an office manager assigns tasks to the right employees, a router network directs tokens to the correct expert subnetworks to save computational power.

cards.title: Meet the AI Traffic Cop
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how giant AI models can answer questions about history, write code, and solve math problems in a split second? They don't use their whole 'brain' for every single word. Instead, they use a clever traffic cop called a router!

cards.title: What is a Router Network?
cards.body: A router network is a decision-making layer in Mixture of Experts (MoE) AI models. It looks at incoming tokens (words, numbers, or code) and decides which expert AI sub-network is best suited to handle them. Instead of activating the entire brain, only the selected experts work on the task, saving a massive amount of computing power!

cards.title: The Sorting Office
cards.body: Imagine you run a giant post office. Instead of every worker trying to read and deliver every single package, you have a smart mail sorter at the front door. This sorter quickly looks at each package's address and sends it directly to the local delivery specialist for that specific neighborhood. The sorter doesn't deliver the package itself; it just makes sure it gets to the right helper!

cards.title: Routing in Action
cards.body: When you type a prompt like 'Solve 5x + 3 = 18 and translate it to Spanish,' the router network splits the prompt. It routes the equation tokens to the 'Math Expert' and the translation tokens to the 'Spanish Expert'. This ensures high-quality answers without wasting energy.

cards.title: Do all experts work?
cards.body: People often think that in a Mixture of Experts model, every expert analyzes every word to vote on the best answer. In reality, the router network makes a quick decision and only sends the word to one or two experts, keeping the rest of the model asleep to save energy!

cards.title: Router vs. No Router
cards.body: Instead of wasting computer power by letting every expert read every word, the router directs traffic to the correct specialist.

left.text: All experts process every single word. Extremely slow and uses too much compute.

right.text: Only the best-suited experts get activated. Fast, efficient, and saves energy.

exercises.prompt: What is the main job of a router network in an MoE model?

options.text: To evaluate each token and send it to the most appropriate expert.
To connect the AI to the physical internet.
To train the experts from scratch using web data.
To block spam messages from reaching the user.

exercises.explain: The router network acts as a traffic director, sending math tasks to math experts and grammar tasks to language experts, which saves valuable computing power.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The router network forces every single expert in the model to process every token.
exercises.explain: The router network only activates the specific experts needed for the tokens, keeping the other experts inactive and saving energy.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence about Mixture of Experts (MoE) routing.
exercises.sentence: To save compute, the _____ network evaluates each token and sends it to the best expert.
exercises.explain: The router network acts as the gatekeeper, sending each token to the expert best suited for the task.

exercises.prompt: Which of these demonstrates a Router Network?

options.text: A system that directs a geometry question specifically to a geometry-trained expert.
A system that broadcasts a math question to all 100 experts at the same time.
A database that stores answers to math questions.

exercises.explain: Option A shows a router network in action: it evaluates the incoming math input and sends it to the specialized math expert, rather than routing it randomly or to everyone.

exercises.prompt: You are designing a high-speed Mixture of Experts (MoE) system. How does the router network help keep the system fast and efficient?

options.text: It forces every expert to process every token at the same time.
It directs each token to the most specialized expert, saving compute.
It translates human language into computer code.
It shuts down the model when it runs out of memory.

exercises.explain: A router network acts as the switchboard operator, sending each token (like code, math, or translation) to the expert network that is trained specifically for that job, saving massive amounts of compute time.
